message:
feat(trace,dispatch): nested trace context infrastructure and BF16/F16 dtype support

Add nested trace simulation API and integrate it into the dispatch
pipeline for transform composition verification:

- fj-trace: introduce NestedTraceFrameSummary and NestedTraceSummary
  structs that capture per-frame transform identity, trace ID, nesting
  depth, and equation/variable counts. Add simulate_nested_trace_contexts()
  which opens one subtrace per transform (outer-to-inner) then closes
  them (inner-to-outer) to verify stack discipline, returning a typed
  summary. Add pop_subtrace_closed() to close a subtrace and return a
  ClosedJaxpr instead of just the trace ID. Change build_closed_jaxpr
  from consuming self to &mut self to support the iterative close
  pattern. Expose nesting_depth() and next_tracer_id_hint() accessors.

- fj-trace: extend ShapedArray::from_value to handle BF16Bits and
  F16Bits literal variants. Add F16 and BF16 cases to promote_dtype()
  with correct widening semantics (half-types promote to F32 when mixed).
  Wire ReduceAnd/ReduceOr/ReduceXor into infer_reduce_sum shape
  inference (they follow the same axis-elimination logic).

- fj-dispatch: add fj-trace dependency and call
  simulate_nested_trace_contexts at the top of dispatch() to validate
  the transform stack before execution. Emit a nested_trace_depth
  evidence signal into the forensic ledger with max depth and per-frame
  trace identifiers.

- fj-trace/tests: add nested_trace_contexts.rs integration test suite
  covering jit(grad), vmap(jit), grad(grad), context isolation,
  subtrace open/close discipline, variable numbering monotonicity,
  transform evidence cross-validation with fj-core, and an e2e oracle
  that writes golden data to artifacts/.

- artifacts: add e2e golden data and oracle logs for nested trace
  conformance scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+{
+  "schema_version": "frankenjax.e2e.nested_traces.v1",
+  "scenario": "e2e_nested_traces_oracle",
+  "generated_at_unix_ms": 0,
+  "cases": [
+    {
+      "transform_stack": ["jit", "grad"],
+      "nesting_depth": 3,
+      "inner_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "outer_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "oracle_match": true,
+      "pass": true
+    },
+    {
+      "transform_stack": ["vmap", "grad"],
+      "nesting_depth": 3,
+      "inner_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "outer_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "oracle_match": true,
+      "pass": true
+    },
+    {
+      "transform_stack": ["grad", "grad"],
+      "nesting_depth": 3,
+      "inner_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "outer_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "oracle_match": true,
+      "pass": true
+    },
+    {
+      "transform_stack": ["vmap", "jit"],
+      "nesting_depth": 3,
+      "inner_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "outer_jaxpr_eqns": 0,
+      "oracle_match": true,
+      "pass": true
+    }
+  ]
+}
